perturbation.original_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees, other individuals who are under the direct control of NatWest and work full time on NatWest premises (an "Individual Consultant"), -- all of whom --------------------- shall have a need to know said Confidential Information and who have executed a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend for only five years with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees, other individuals who are under the direct control of NatWest and work full time on NatWest premises (an "Individual Consultant"), -- all of whom --------------------- shall have a need to know said Confidential Information and who have executed a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality for InterTrust Technology. The original text states that the confidentiality extends 'in perpetuity', while the modified text limits it to 'five years.' This contradiction creates uncertainty about how long Recipient is obligated to keep InterTrust Technology confidential, leading to potential legal disputes. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 1 which mentions 'In consideration for such Confidential Information being provided to Recipient, Recipient agrees to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to a Technology Development, Marketing and License Agreement dated August 18, 1998 between InterTrust and NatWest (the "License Agreement"), ----------------- InterTrust may disclose to NatWest certain confidential information including technical information embodied in and/or associated with InterTrust's InterTrust Technology including, without limitation, software products and/or other developments related to distributed, secure rights and/or event management, associated designs, inventions, plans, and other information (the "Confidential ------------ Information"), all of which such information shall conspicuously be marked with ----------- a notice or legend with the phrase "Confidential", as provided in the License Agreement. In consideration for such Confidential Information being provided to Recipient, Recipient agrees to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Disclosure of InterTrust Confidential Information to Recipient, and use and disclosure of Confidential Information received by Recipient, shall occur only in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of confidentiality for InterTrust Technology from 'in perpetuity' to 'five years'. The contradicted text states that the Recipient agrees to be bound by the terms of the agreement in consideration for the Confidential Information being provided. This creates a direct contradiction, as the terms regarding the duration of confidentiality are now inconsistent, leading to uncertainty about the obligations, specifically regarding how long the recipient must keep the information confidential, as the perpetuity is contradicted by the new 5 year rule. This falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above.
perturbation.explanation: The change in wording from "shall not" to "may" introduces a complete contradiction. The original text clearly prohibits disclosing Confidential Information to unauthorized individuals. The modified text now allows such disclosure. This creates an ambiguity about whether the recipient is allowed to share the information, violating the purpose of the NDA. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 3 which mentions 'The undersigned understands and acknowledges that the unauthorized use of Confidential Information may cause InterTrust very substantial damage'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for no other purposes whatsoever. The undersigned understands and acknowledges that the unauthorized use of Confidential Information may cause InterTrust very substantial damage, for which damages may be impossible to measure or inadequate to compensate. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that if he or she breaches or threatens breach of any of such sections, InterTrust will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction against him or her, from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened <PAGE> CONFIDENTIAL breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement, and Recipient hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance under such circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient 'shall not' disclose confidential information, while the changed text says the Recipient 'may' disclose confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, 'The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for no other purposes whatsoever.' By changing 'shall not' to 'may,' the modified text creates a strong contradiction and ambiguity about whether the recipient is allowed to share the information.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public through no breach of any of the undersigned's obligations under this Agreement, or NatWest's or any other NatWest employee's, obligations of confidentiality to NatWest and/or to InterTrust; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust, as evidenced through written documentation; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient without any reliance on or use of any InterTrust Confidential Information, as demonstrated through written documentation; or (iv) shall have been rightfully supplied to Recipient, with no obligation of confidentiality or non-use from a third party without any breach of any obligation of confidentiality to InterTrust or NatWest (as applicable) as of the date such information is so supplied.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient; or (iv) shall have been rightfully supplied to Recipient, with no obligation of confidentiality or non-use from a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "through no breach of any of the undersigned's obligations under this Agreement, or NatWest's or any other NatWest employee's, obligations of confidentiality to NatWest and/or to InterTrust;" from condition (i) and  the phrase "as demonstrated through written documentation" from conditions (ii) and (iii), this makes it unclear when the confidentiality restrictions apply. The original clause (i) states, the confidentiality restrictions of this agreement shall not apply if information becomes available to the public through no breach of obligations under the NDA. Changing this, allows for scenarios where breaches are not a matter. Section 2 also mentions that 'Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees, other individuals who are under the direct control of NatWest and work full time on NatWest premises (an "Individual Consultant"), -- all of whom --------------------- shall have a need to know said Confidential Information and who have executed a copy of this Agreement. Recipient shall not, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above. Furthermore, without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, the Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country. The undersigned will not use the Confidential Information in any manner that is not authorized by NatWest and in accordance with the License Agreement and the undersigned will use the Confidential Information solely in the exercise of NatWest's rights as provided by the License Agreement. Under no circumstances will any information subject to the export or import laws of any jurisdiction be transferred pursuant to their agreement without proper prior certification and notification of appropriate regulatory offices in applicable jurisdictions and InterTrust.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes key clauses from the confidentiality restrictions exceptions. Specifically, removing "through no breach of any of the undersigned's obligations under this Agreement..." from exception (i) and "as demonstrated through written documentation" from exceptions (ii) and (iii) creates ambiguity regarding when confidentiality restrictions apply. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states, 'Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence'. The removal of the 'no breach' clause now allows for information released due to a breach to fall under the exception, directly conflicting with the obligation to keep all confidential information confidential, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: If any provision or portion thereof in this Agreement shall be found or be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable in any jurisdiction in which this Agreement is being performed, then this Agreement shall nevertheless be given full force and effect without said provision or portion.
perturbation.changed_text: If any provision or portion thereof in this Agreement shall be found or be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable in any jurisdiction in which this Agreement is being performed, then this Agreement shall be considered null and void.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original statement in Section 6. Originally, if a provision was found to be unenforceable, the remainder of the agreement would still be valid. The new definition states the entire agreement is null and void. This presents an in-text contradiction, specifically considering the clause regarding the governing law and jurisdiction specified in the same Section: 'This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the United States of America and the Commonwealth of Virginia, USA, without reference to conflicts of laws principles'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any provision or portion thereof in this Agreement shall be found or be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable in any jurisdiction in which this Agreement is being performed, then this Agreement shall be considered null and void. This Agreement may not be modified except by written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the United States of America and the Commonwealth of Virginia, USA, without reference to conflicts of laws principles. All disputes arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia, Alexandria Division Virginia, or if jurisdiction does not properly lie in such court, the Commonwealth courts in Alexandria, Virginia. The parties consent to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such court and waive any argument that jurisdiction or venue in such court is improper or inconvenient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that if any provision is found unenforceable, the entire agreement becomes null and void. The contradicted text, taken from the same original section, includes clauses on governing law, jurisdiction, and modification procedures. If the entire agreement is null and void, these clauses also become void, creating a direct contradiction because these clauses are intended to remain in effect even if other provisions are unenforceable. The original text allowed the agreement to remain in effect even if a single portion was found to be invalid. Now the agreement will be void, directly contradicting the intention that the governing law and jurisdiction will still be in effect, causing an in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
